summary.brief: Boesen v. Brown. Medical malpractice case involving oncology.

evidence_index.text: Mrs. Boesen began experiencing tongue irritation in early 2016. In February, her dentist noted a 'soft tissue lesion of the left lateral border of the tongue' and suspected an allergic reaction.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: On August 30, 2016, Dr. Brown examined Mrs. Boesen and noted a 'whitish plaque approximately 4 cm by 1 cm of the left lateral/ventral border' of her tongue.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The biopsy was sent to LabCorp for analysis and returned a diagnosis of 'lichenoid mucositis' and stated that 'differential diagnostic possibilities include lichen planus and lichenoid drug eruption.'
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Dr. Sciubba performed a biopsy, which returned a diagnosis of 'invasive squamous cell carcinoma.'
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Dr. Bernstein’s testimony in full context establishes that he believes Dr. Brown violated an established standard—as he put it, 'a maxim in oral pathology'—when Dr. Brown did not re-biopsy Mrs. Boesen’s lesion.
evidence_index.speaker: Expert testimony

evidence_index.text: The Court DENIES Defendant’s Motion for Summary Judgment.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

initial_state.history_of_present_illness: Mrs. Boesen began experiencing tongue irritation in early 2016. In February, her dentist noted a 'soft tissue lesion of the left lateral border of the tongue' and suspected an allergic reaction. Symptoms reappeared in May, leading to a referral to an oral surgery doctor who evaluated her in June and July, concluding the lesion was due to trauma or an autoimmune issue. In mid-July, another doctor noted irritation on the left ventral side of the tongue, which had been a source of pain for about 7 months.

focused_exam.oral: Whitish plaque approximately 4 cm by 1 cm on the left lateral/ventral border of the tongue

initial_state.initial_working_problem: Tongue lesion with differential including autoimmune condition or trauma

requestables.name: Biopsy of tongue lesion
requestables.description: Punch biopsy of the tongue lesion to rule out squamous cell carcinoma

reveal.result_summary: Lichenoid mucositis; differential includes lichen planus and lichenoid drug eruption
reveal.clinical_significance: No evidence of high grade dysplasia
reveal.decision_impact: Did not prompt further immediate investigation

requestables.name: Re-biopsy of tongue lesion
requestables.description: Re-biopsy of the lesion on the tongue to confirm diagnosis

timeline_phases.name: Initial Presentation
timeline_phases.description: Patient presents with tongue irritation and pain

key_events.event: Initial dentist visit and referral to oral surgery

timeline_phases.name: Biopsy and Diagnosis
timeline_phases.description: Biopsy performed and initial diagnosis made

timeline_phases.name: Subsequent Diagnosis and Treatment
timeline_phases.description: Diagnosis of squamous cell carcinoma and subsequent treatment

key_events.event: Diagnosis of invasive squamous cell carcinoma

timeline_phases.name: Follow-up and Re-evaluation
timeline_phases.description: Re-biopsy performed after lack of improvement

decision_points.clinical_context: Patient with persistent tongue lesion and pain
decision_points.prompt: What should be the next step in management after the initial biopsy results?

options.description: Perform a second biopsy to confirm diagnosis
options.clinical_reasoning: Persistent symptoms and risk of malignancy warrant further investigation

options.description: Diagnose as lichenoid mucositis and provide steroids

actual_action_defendant.description: Defendant diagnosed lichenoid mucositis and provided steroids
actual_action_defendant.reasoning_stated: Believed condition was non-malignant based on biopsy

expected_action_court.description: A second biopsy should have been performed
expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: Expert testimony indicated further investigation was necessary

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Persistent symptoms and risk factors for malignancy were not adequately addressed
explanation.what_should_have_happened: A second biopsy should have been performed to rule out malignancy
explanation.legal_standard_applied: Standard of care as established by expert testimony

decision_points.clinical_context: Patient with persistent oral lesion not responding to treatment
decision_points.prompt: What should be the next step in management?

options.description: Re-biopsy the lesion due to lack of improvement
options.clinical_reasoning: Persistent lesions require re-biopsy to rule out malignancy

options.description: Continue with current treatment without re-biopsy

actual_action_defendant.description: Defendant did not perform a re-biopsy
actual_action_defendant.reasoning_stated: Defendant continued with treatment without further investigation

expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: Persistent lesions require re-biopsy according to standard practice

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Failure to re-biopsy delayed the diagnosis of cancer
explanation.what_should_have_happened: Re-biopsy should have been performed to confirm or rule out malignancy
explanation.legal_standard_applied: Standard of care as established by expert testimony

patient_outcome.description: Diagnosis of invasive squamous cell carcinoma confirmed

key_findings: Court denied summary judgment, allowing the case to proceed

malpractice_determination.point_of_failure: Failure to biopsy red component and re-biopsy persistent lesion
malpractice_determination.counterfactual: Earlier biopsy could have led to earlier diagnosis

factual_timeline.event: Mrs. Boesen began experiencing tongue irritation
Diagnosis of invasive squamous cell carcinoma
Dr. Brown examined Mrs. Boesen and noted a whitish plaque on her tongue

treatments.outcome: Cancer had not spread to lymph nodes

allegations.allegation: Failure to perform a second biopsy
Failure to biopsy red component and re-biopsy persistent lesion

court_findings.finding: Court denied summary judgment, allowing the case to proceed
court_findings.legal_significance: Case not dismissed, further proceedings required
